title: inline sRGB ICC so PDF/A-3 invoices render in prod
description:
## Summary

`pdf-a3-embed.ts` was reading `website/src/assets/sRGB.icc` at runtime via `readFileSync(fileURLToPath(import.meta.url) + '..')`. Vite/Astro does not track `readFileSync()`-based asset loads, so the file was **never copied into `website/dist/`**. The first invoice render in the deployed pod would have crashed with `ENOENT '../assets/sRGB.icc'`, blowing up the OutputIntent that PDF/A-3 (and Factur-X / XRechnung e-invoicing) requires.

## Fix

- `website/src/lib/srgb-icc.ts`: 3 KB profile inlined as a base64 constant; `Uint8Array` exported. Bundler keeps it, no FS at runtime.
- `website/src/lib/pdf-a3-embed.ts`: imports `SRGB_ICC` directly; drops the `node:fs` / `node:url` / `node:path` imports that existed only for this read.
- `scripts/build-srgb-icc.mjs`: regenerates `srgb-icc.ts` from the canonical `.icc` (run after replacing the profile binary).

`website/src/assets/sRGB.icc` remains as source-of-truth for regeneration; the embedded SHA-256 in the generated `.ts` documents which build of the profile is shipped.

## Verification

- `npx tsc --noEmit` clean.
- `npm run build` succeeds; `dist/server/chunks/invoice-pdf_*.mjs` grew from 7 KB → 30 KB and now contains `SRGB_ICC_BASE64` literal — no more reference to a missing asset path.

## Test plan

- [ ] `task feature:website` (deploy to mentolder + korczewski).
- [ ] Render an invoice on each cluster and confirm the OutputIntent survives (no 5xx, downloaded PDF passes a Factur-X validator).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
